```python
expected = metadata.get("expected_output_bitstring", "01111111001010")
input_a = metadata.get("input_a", "100101")
input_b = metadata.get("input_b", "011010")

assert expected in counts, (expected, counts)

total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots

top_bitstring, top_count = max(counts.items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1])
assert top_bitstring == expected
assert top_count == shots

sum_and_carry = expected[:7]
restored_addend = expected[7:]
assert sum_and_carry == expected_sum_bits
assert restored_addend == input_b

a_val = int(input_a, 2)
b_val = int(input_b, 2)
sum_val = int(sum_and_carry, 2)
assert sum_val == a_val + b_val
```

```python
expected = metadata.get("expected_output_bitstring", "01111111001010")
input_a = metadata.get("input_a", "100101")
input_b = metadata.get("input_b", "011010")
expected_sum = metadata.get("expected_sum_bits", "0111111")

assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"total counts {total} != shots {shots}"

assert set(counts.keys()) == {expected}, f"unexpected measured bitstrings: {set(counts.keys())}"
assert counts[expected] == shots, f"expected all shots to yield {expected}, got {counts}"

bitstring = expected
sum_part = bitstring[:7]
restored_b = bitstring[7:13]
carry_in = bitstring[13]

assert sum_part == expected_sum, f"sum bits {sum_part} != expected {expected_sum}"
assert restored_b == input_b, f"restored addend register {restored_b} != input_b {input_b}"
assert carry_in == "0", f"carry-in bit not restored: {carry_in}"

assert int(expected_sum, 2) == int(input_a, 2) + int(input_b, 2), "sum does not match inputs"
```

```python
expected = metadata.get("expected_output_bitstring", "01111111001010")
input_a = metadata.get("input_a", "100101")
input_b = metadata.get("input_b", "011010")
expected_sum_bits = metadata.get("expected_sum_bits", "0111111")

assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"total counts {total} != shots {shots}"

assert set(counts.keys()) == {expected}, f"unexpected measured bitstrings: {set(counts.keys())}"

bitstring = next(iter(counts))
assert bitstring == expected

sum_part = bitstring[:7]
restored_b_part = bitstring[7:13]
carry_in_part = bitstring[13]

assert sum_part == expected_sum_bits, f"sum bits {sum_part} != expected {expected_sum_bits}"
assert restored_b_part == input_b, f"restored addend register {restored_b_part} != input_b {input_b}"
assert carry_in_part == "0", f"carry-in qubit not restored: {carry_in_part}"

assert int(sum_part, 2) == int(input_a, 2) + int(input_b, 2), "measured sum has wrong numeric value"
assert counts[expected] == shots, f"expected all shots on {expected}, got {counts[expected]} of {shots}"
```

```python
expected = metadata.get("expected_output_bitstring", "01111111001010")
input_a = metadata.get("input_a", "100101")
input_b = metadata.get("input_b", "011010")
expected_sum_bits = metadata.get("expected_sum_bits", "0111111")

assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"total counted shots {total} != shots {shots}"

assert set(counts.keys()) == {expected}, f"unexpected measured bitstrings: {set(counts.keys())}"
bitstring = next(iter(counts))

assert bitstring == expected, f"expected output {expected}, got {bitstring}"

# With measure(qi, ci) for all i, the count key is c13...c0, so:
# q13..q7 should hold carry-out + 6-bit sum, and q6..q1 should restore input_a.
assert bitstring[:7] == expected_sum_bits, f"sum/carry bits mismatch: {bitstring[:7]} != {expected_sum_bits}"
assert bitstring[7:13] == input_a, f"addend register not restored: {bitstring[7:13]} != {input_a}"
assert bitstring[-1] == "0", f"carry-in bit not restored to 0: got {bitstring[-1]}"

# Sanity check against classical addition.
a = int(input_a, 2)
b = int(input_b, 2)
assert format(a + b, "07b") == expected_sum_bits
```

```python
expected = metadata.get("expected_output_bitstring", "01111111001010")
input_a = metadata.get("input_a", "100101")
input_b = metadata.get("input_b", "011010")
expected_sum_bits = metadata.get("expected_sum_bits", "0111111")

assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"total counts {total} != shots {shots}"

assert set(counts.keys()) == {expected}, f"unexpected measured bitstrings: {set(counts.keys())}"
```

```python
bitstring = next(iter(counts))
assert bitstring == expected

# With measure(i, i) for all qubits, the returned bitstring is c13...c0 = q13...q0.
# Expected layout: [q13]=restored carry-in, [q12..q6]=sum bits, [q5..q0]=restored addend register.
assert bitstring[0] == "0", "carry-in bit should be restored to 0"
assert bitstring[1:8] == expected_sum_bits, f"sum bits mismatch: {bitstring[1:8]} != {expected_sum_bits}"
assert bitstring[8:] == input_a, f"addend register mismatch: {bitstring[8:]} != {input_a}"

# Cross-check against classical addition: input_a + input_b = expected_sum_bits.
a = int(input_a, 2)
b = int(input_b, 2)
assert format(a + b, "07b") == expected_sum_bits
```

```python
expected = metadata.get("expected_output_bitstring", "01111111001010")
input_a = metadata.get("input_a", "100101")
input_b = metadata.get("input_b", "011010")
expected_sum_bits = metadata.get("expected_sum_bits", "0111111")
```

```python
assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"total counts {total} != shots {shots}"

assert expected in counts, f"expected output {expected} not found in counts"
assert counts[expected] == shots, f"expected all shots on {expected}, got {counts}"

bitstring = expected
assert len(bitstring) == 14

# Qiskit count keys are ordered c13..c0, so split as:
# [q13][q12..q7][q6..q0]
carry_out = bitstring[0]
b_register = bitstring[1:7]
low_register = bitstring[7:]

assert carry_out + b_register == expected_sum_bits, (
    f"sum/carry mismatch: got {carry_out + b_register}, expected {expected_sum_bits}"
)
assert low_register == input_a[::-1], (
    f"addend register not restored: got {low_register}, expected {input_a[::-1]}"
)

# Also verify the arithmetic meaning directly.
a_val = int(input_a, 2)
b_val = int(input_b, 2)
sum_val = a_val + b_val
assert format(sum_val, "07b") == expected_sum_bits
```

```python
expected = metadata.get("expected_output_bitstring", "01111111001010")
input_a = metadata.get("input_a", "100101")
expected_sum = metadata.get("expected_sum_bits", "0111111")

assert isinstance(counts, dict) and len(counts) > 0
assert expected in counts, f"Expected output {expected} not found in counts: {counts}"
assert counts[expected] == shots, f"Expected all {shots} shots to be {expected}, got {counts}"

measured = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert measured == expected

sum_bits = measured[:7]
restored_a = measured[8:]
carry_in_bit = measured[7]

assert sum_bits == expected_sum, f"Incorrect sum bits: {sum_bits} != {expected_sum}"
assert carry_in_bit == "1", f"Carry-in/addend boundary bit not restored as expected: {carry_in_bit}"
assert restored_a == input_a, f"Addend register not restored: {restored_a} != {input_a}"
```

```python
expected = metadata.get("expected_output_bitstring", "01111111001010")
input_a = metadata.get("input_a", "100101")
input_b = metadata.get("input_b", "011010")
expected_sum_bits = metadata.get("expected_sum_bits", "0111111")

assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"total counted shots {total} != shots {shots}"
```

```python
assert expected in counts, f"expected output bitstring {expected} not found in counts"
assert counts[expected] == shots, f"expected all shots to measure {expected}, got {counts}"

# Also verify the output structure: [sum(7 bits)][restored addend B(6 bits)][restored carry-in(1 bit)]
sum_bits = expected[:7]
restored_b = expected[7:13]
restored_cin = expected[13]

assert sum_bits == expected_sum_bits, f"sum bits mismatch: {sum_bits} != {expected_sum_bits}"
assert restored_b == input_b, f"addend register not restored: {restored_b} != {input_b}"
assert restored_cin == "0", f"carry-in bit not restored to 0: got {restored_cin}"

# Cross-check arithmetic from fixed inputs
assert format(int(input_a, 2) + int(input_b, 2), "07b") == expected_sum_bits
```

```python
expected = metadata.get("expected_output_bitstring", "01111111001010")
input_a = metadata.get("input_a", "100101")
input_b = metadata.get("input_b", "011010")
expected_sum_bits = metadata.get("expected_sum_bits", "0111111")

assert isinstance(counts, dict) and counts, "counts must be a non-empty dict"

total = sum(counts.values())
assert total == shots, f"total counts {total} != shots {shots}"
assert set(counts.keys()) == {expected}, f"unexpected measured bitstrings: {set(counts.keys())}"

bitstring = next(iter(counts))
assert counts[bitstring] == shots, "expected deterministic output for fixed classical inputs"

assert bitstring == expected, f"got {bitstring}, expected {expected}"

# q13..q7 should hold the 7-bit sum, q6..q1 should restore addend A, and q0 carry-in should be 0.
sum_part = bitstring[:7]
restored_a_part = bitstring[7:13]
carry_in_part = bitstring[13]

assert sum_part == expected_sum_bits, f"sum bits {sum_part} != expected {expected_sum_bits}"
assert restored_a_part == input_a, f"restored addend register {restored_a_part} != input_a {input_a}"
assert carry_in_part == "0", f"carry-in bit not restored: {carry_in_part}"

# Cross-check against classical arithmetic: 6-bit A + 6-bit B -> 7-bit sum.
a_val = int(input_a, 2)
b_val = int(input_b, 2)
classical_sum_bits = format(a_val + b_val, "07b")
assert classical_sum_bits == expected_sum_bits, (
    f"classical sum {classical_sum_bits} != expected_sum_bits {expected_sum_bits}"
)
```
